entries.name: code-reviewer
entries.description: Code Reviewer — systematically identifies bugs, security issues, test coverage gaps, and style violations

entries.name: database-reviewer
entries.description: Database Reviewer — schema design, migrations, indexes, constraints, transaction boundaries

entries.name: security-auditor
entries.description: Security Auditor — OWASP checks, dependency scanning, secrets detection, threat paths

entries.name: systematic-debugger
entries.description: Systematic Debugger — evidence-driven root cause analysis, hypothesis testing

entries.name: ultrathink-debugger
entries.description: Ultrathink Debugger — deep reasoning for gnarly bugs, 3+ level traces

entries.name: Architect
entries.description: Strategic Architecture & Debugging Advisor (Opus, READ-ONLY)

entries.name: Code Reviewer
entries.description: Expert code review specialist with severity-rated feedback, logic defect detection, SOLID principle checks, style, performance, and quality strategy

entries.name: Critic
entries.description: Work plan and code review expert — thorough, structured, multi-perspective (Opus)

entries.name: Debugger
entries.description: Root-cause analysis, regression isolation, stack trace analysis, build/compilation error resolution

entries.name: Document Specialist
entries.description: External Documentation & Reference Specialist

entries.name: Executor
entries.description: Focused task executor for implementation work (Sonnet)

entries.name: Git Master
entries.description: Git expert for atomic commits, rebasing, and history management with style detection

entries.name: Planner
entries.description: Strategic planning consultant with interview workflow (Opus)

entries.name: Test Engineer
entries.description: Test strategy, integration/e2e coverage, flaky test hardening, TDD workflows

entries.name: Verifier
entries.description: Verification strategy, evidence-based completion checks, test adequacy

entries.name: Claude Code Power User
entries.description: Opinionated bundle for Claude Code users — prompting discipline, known agent gotchas, worktree workflow, plus the two MCP servers you'll reach for most often.

entries.name: Discord Bot Development
entries.description: Opinionated bundle for building a TypeScript Discord bot (e.g. Lucky). Pairs prompting/testing skills with the servers you'll reach for — GitHub for PRs, Redis for state inspection, Sentry for production triage.

entries.name: Agent Observability
entries.description: AI coding agents produce outputs that look correct but fail in subtle ways: - Hallucinated function names that pass review but fail at runtime - Tool call sequences that work once but regress under different inputs - Prompt changes that improve one case but silently degrade another

entries.name: AI Observability in Production
entries.description: Production AI systems require deep observability into model performance, cost, and data quality. This pattern covers instrumentation, alerting, and data quality monitoring for LLM-powered applications.

entries.name: Benchmark Reality Gap
entries.description: SWE-bench and similar curated benchmarks overestimate agent capability by 50%+ in real-world deployment. The gap emerges from benchmark framing: clean task descriptions with sufficient context don't resemble actual user queries, which are ambiguous, under-specified, and embedded in noisy ticket systems. Closing the gap requires domain-specific evaluation sets grounded in real user behavior.

entries.name: AI Skill Stewardship
entries.description: Coding agents amplify what you already are. They don't substitute for what you aren't.

entries.name: Context Management for AI Coding
entries.description: ### Progressive Loading Don't read entire files upfront. Build context incrementally: 1. Start with project structure (glob, ls) 2. Read specific functions (grep for signatures, then read targeted lines) 3. Load full files only when editing them

entries.name: Security Practices for AI-Assisted Development
entries.description: ### Never in shell config Move API keys from ~/.bashrc/config.fish to a separate file: ``bash # ~/.config/fish/conf.d/secrets.fish (chmod 600) set -gx API_KEY "your-key" ``

entries.name: AI-Assisted Development Workflow
entries.description: `` feature/my-feature → PR to main → automated deploy ``

entries.name: Cost-Aware Routing
entries.description: Model choice is now a cost/quality tradeoff, not a binary "do I need reasoning" decision. This pattern extends packages/core/patterns/multi-model-routing.md with explicit economics: token pricing, caching strategy, and output-format optimization that recovers 40-60% of spend.

entries.name: LLM Evaluation Patterns
entries.description: Measure before you ship. Intuition about model quality is unreliable — score it systematically. This guide covers evaluation frameworks, golden datasets, regression testing, and production monitoring for LLM-powered features.

entries.name: MCP Tool Lazy Loading
entries.description: How Claude Code lazily resolves MCP tool schemas via ToolSearch, and how that changes which tools you can call at any moment.

entries.name: Multi-Model Routing
entries.description: Using a frontier model for every task wastes money and time. Using a weak model for hard tasks wastes your time fixing its output. The optimal strategy routes tasks to the right capability tier.

entries.name: OpenTelemetry GenAI Semantic Conventions
entries.description: Vendor lock-in to observability layers has cost you money. Decoupling telemetry collection from analysis backends lets you migrate from Datadog to Grafana without rewriting instrumentation. OpenTelemetry GenAI Semantic Conventions (GA March 2026) is the standard way forward.

entries.name: Agency persona
entries.description: Delivery Agency is a compact services-oriented company template. It is designed for client projects where discovery, execution, QA, and communication must all move in sync.

entries.name: Fullstack Forge persona
entries.description: Fullstack Forge is a full-service software development consultancy built from 66 specialized skills. The company uses a hub-and-spoke workflow: the CTO routes work to the appropriate team lead, and team leads assign tasks to specialists with deep domain expertise.

entries.name: Open Source Maintainer persona
entries.description: Open Source Maintainer Collective is a lightweight company template for maintainers who need structure around releases, documentation, community support, and issue triage.

entries.name: Solopreneur persona
entries.description: Solopreneur Studio is a compact agent company for a founder-led product. It keeps the org simple: one founder/operator drives priorities, one product engineer ships, and one growth operator closes the loop with users.

entries.name: Startup Mvp persona
entries.description: Startup MVP Lab is a small company template for early-stage startups. It separates product direction, implementation, and launch so teams move fast without blurring ownership.

entries.name: RAG Architecture Patterns
entries.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) connects LLMs to external knowledge. This guide covers production-ready patterns for building, debugging, and scaling RAG pipelines.

entries.name: Reasoning-Model Prompting
entries.description: Reasoning models (Claude Opus 4 with extended thinking, o3/o3-mini) operate at a different cost and latency boundary than standard models. They trade 2-10x latency and 3-5x token cost for dramatically higher first-pass quality on hard problems. This requires a different prompting discipline: less scaffolding, more constraints, explicit reasoning budgets.

entries.name: Streaming Orchestration
entries.description: You send a prompt and wait. You don't know if the agent is stuck, hallucinating, or burning through tokens on the wrong task. When something goes wrong, you can't tell where. Budget overruns happen silently.

entries.name: Auto Context Pack
entries.description: Claude Code hook: auto-context-pack

entries.name: Auto Format
entries.description: Auto-format code after Claude edits Ensures consistent formatting across all file types

entries.name: Automation Orchestrator
entries.description: Claude Code Hook: Automation Orchestrator Intelligent automation orchestration for development workflows

entries.name: Block Destructive
entries.description: Block destructive commands hook for Claude Code Prevents dangerous operations that could cause data loss

entries.name: Context Backup
entries.description: Context backup hook for Claude Code Backs up important context before compaction

entries.name: Context Injector
entries.description: Context injection hook for Claude Code Injects relevant context based on user prompt analysis

entries.name: Context Optimizer
entries.description: Claude Code Hook: Context Optimizer Intelligent context optimization for token efficiency and performance

entries.name: Message Counter
entries.description: message-counter.sh — UserPromptSubmit hook Tracks message count per session. Auto-generates per-project handoff at limit threshold. Cost: 0 tokens on normal messages. Only prints at thresholds 15, 20, 25, 30+.

entries.name: Post Compact
entries.description: Claude Code hook: post-compact

entries.name: Post Edit Optimization
entries.description: Post-Edit Token Optimization Hook Optimizes context and token usage after file edits

entries.name: Pre Commit Validation
entries.description: Pre-commit validation hook for UIForge MCP Runs essential checks before allowing commits

entries.name: Pre Compact
entries.description: Pre-Compact Snapshot

entries.name: Protect Files
entries.description: Claude Code hook: protect-files

entries.name: Rtk Rewrite
entries.description: rtk-hook-version: 3 RTK Claude Code hook — rewrites commands to use rtk for token savings. Requires: rtk >= 0.23.0, jq This is a thin delegating hook: all rewrite logic lives in `rtk rewrite`, which is the single source of truth (src/discover/registry.rs). To add or change rewrite rules, edit the Rust registry — not this file. Exit code protocol for `rtk rewrite`: 0 + stdout Rewrite found, no deny/ask rule matched → auto-allow 1 No RTK equivalent → pass through unchanged 2 De

entries.name: Secret Detection
entries.description: Secret detection hook for UIForge MCP Scans for hardcoded secrets and sensitive data

entries.name: Security Scan
entries.description: Security scanning hook for UIForge MCP Runs comprehensive security checks on code changes

entries.name: Session Setup
entries.description: Claude Code session setup hook (Fish version) Optimizes session for productivity and token efficiency

entries.name: Session Start Optimization
entries.description: Session Start Context Optimization Hook Optimizes context when starting a new session

entries.name: Test Auto Context Pack
entries.description: Test suite for auto-context-pack hook

entries.name: Token Usage Monitor
entries.description: Token Usage Monitoring Hook Monitors and reports on token usage patterns

entries.name: Validate Command
entries.description: Claude Code hook: validate-command

entries.name: Validate Handoff
entries.description: Claude Code hook: validate-handoff

entries.name: Validate Secrets
entries.description: Validate secrets and enforce zero-secrets policy Ensures no secrets are committed or processed

entries.name: Validate Tests
entries.description: Validate tests after code changes Ensures test coverage and quality standards

entries.name: Workflow Logger
entries.description: Workflow logging hook for Claude Code Tracks workflow events and productivity metrics

entries.name: Workflow Validator
entries.description: Workflow validation hook for Claude Code Validates workflows and ensures compliance with Forge Space standards

entries.name: Context7
entries.description: Fetches current documentation for libraries, frameworks, SDKs, and APIs. Prefer over web search for library docs — training data rots fast.

entries.name: Docker
entries.description: Inspect containers, fetch logs, run one-off containers, deploy compose stacks. Useful for homelab / local-stack debugging from the editor.

entries.name: GitHub
entries.description: Access GitHub PRs, issues, reviews, CI checks, commits, branches, and releases via MCP. Preferred over raw `gh` for structured reads.

entries.name: Kubernetes
entries.description: Read + edit live cluster state — pods, nodes, events, logs, resources. Use only against clusters you expect the model to touch.

entries.name: Linear
entries.description: Read + write Linear issues, projects, cycles, comments, and documents. Uses a local stdio wrapper against Linear's GraphQL API — your API key never leaves your machine.

entries.name: Memory (Knowledge Graph)
entries.description: Official reference knowledge-graph memory server — entities, relations, observations persisted across sessions. Good default for project memory.

entries.name: Playwright
entries.description: Browser automation via Playwright — navigate, click, fill forms, take screenshots, inspect console/network. Use for UI testing and scraping.

entries.name: Redis
entries.description: Get / set / list / delete keys in a Redis instance. Useful for inspecting queue state, caches, and ad-hoc debugging.

entries.name: Sentry
entries.description: Query Sentry issues, events, and releases from an MCP client. Useful for triaging production errors inline.

entries.name: Serena
entries.description: Semantic code navigation — symbol search, referencing-symbols, symbol-body read/rewrite. Cheaper than full-file reads on large codebases.

entries.name: Adt Add
entries.description: Meta-adder for toolkit artifacts. Single entry point to add a skill, pattern, best-practice, hook, agent, or guide with correct frontmatter + sync to 4 publish targets.

entries.name: Adt Auto Invoke
entries.description: Meta-skill — defines when to apply other skills automatically without being asked

entries.name: Adt Bilingual Readme Sync
entries.description: Keep EN/PT README.md pairs in parity across a repo (and across twin repos like ai-dev-toolkit + ai-dev-toolkit-pt-br).

entries.name: Adt Build Fix
entries.description: Local build-error triage. Detects common compile errors and suggests fixes

entries.name: Adt Checkpoint
entries.description: Git-level WIP safety net. Stashes uncommitted work, tags, and pushes to remote

entries.name: Adt Compress Assets
entries.description: Losslessly compress PNG and SVG assets in place and generate .webp siblings for every raster image. Use when the user asks to "compress assets", "optimize images", "shrink the assets folder", or "convert to webp". Runs a deterministic pipeline via oxipng + svgo + cwebp + ImageMagick.

entries.name: Adt Context
entries.description: Optimize context window usage — compact stale data, compress via summarization, preserve active state

entries.name: Adt Context Hygiene
entries.description: Keep sessions focused and efficient — prune stale outputs, preserve active state, recommend compaction

entries.name: Adt Cost
entries.description: Track and report token usage and estimated cost per session, agent, and phase

entries.name: Adt Dispatch
entries.description: Spawn parallel subtasks, assign to worker agents, collect results

entries.name: Adt Eval
entries.description: Evaluate LLM outputs systematically — benchmarks, automated metrics, human preference, and regression tracking

entries.name: Adt Fallback
entries.description: Graceful degradation when primary approach fails. Try alternates in priority order; record which path succeeded for future routing.

entries.name: Adt Learn
entries.description: Auto-extract reusable patterns from the current session into skills or memory

entries.name: Adt Loop
entries.description: Autonomous development cycle — plan, implement, test, review, fix, commit, and PR without stopping

entries.name: Adt Mcp Doctor
entries.description: Diagnose and repair failing MCP servers. Captures real launch tracebacks, identifies stale tool/config references, prunes the bad entries, and reconnects. Use when `claude mcp list` shows `Failed to connect`, after upgrading an MCP server (Serena, @modelcontextprotocol/server-*, or vendor MCPs), or when an MCP that worked yesterday silently stopped responding.

entries.name: Adt Mcp Health
entries.description: Validate live MCP provider health — separate config issues from auth and connectivity failures

entries.name: Adt Mcp Patterns
entries.description: Build robust, secure MCP servers — tool schemas, stateful interactions, async patterns, error handling, and testing

entries.name: Adt Mcp Readiness
entries.description: Check whether MCP-backed workflows are usable on this machine

entries.name: Adt Memory
entries.description: Persist decisions, preferences, and project state across sessions using structured memory types

entries.name: Adt Model Serving
entries.description: Choose and configure inference servers (vLLM, TGI, Ollama) — quantization, batching, scaling, and serving patterns

entries.name: Adt Multi Agent
entries.description: Orchestrate teams of specialized agents — DAG execution, task distribution, state sharing, failure recovery

entries.name: Adt Orchestrate
entries.description: Coordinate multiple subagents/worktrees for parallel workstreams. Decomposes larger tasks into independent sub-tasks, dispatches each to a dedicated agent.

entries.name: Adt Plan
entries.description: Analyze the codebase and create a structured implementation plan before writing any code

entries.name: Adt Plan Change
entries.description: Plan a code change before editing — identify files, sequence steps, set verification criteria

entries.name: Adt Plugin Audit
entries.description: Diff the Anthropic `claude-plugins-official` marketplace against installed plugins and surface the delta, filtering known-unwanted entries (language LSPs you don't use, SaaS integrations you don't use, sample/demo plugins).

entries.name: Adt Rag
entries.description: Build and debug Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipelines — chunking, embedding, retrieval, reranking

entries.name: Adt Rag Context Pack
entries.description: Build a task-aware context bundle (relevant code + applicable standards + related past decisions) via the local RAG index, capped at a token budget. Use at the start of any implementation/refactor/debug task instead of reading files blindly. Replaces "read whole file" with "retrieve the function + callers + rules + prior ADR."

entries.name: Adt Rag Recall
entries.description: Semantic-search personal knowledge (memory, plans, handoffs, skills, Codex rules) via the local RAG index at ~/.claude/rag-index/. Use when a query is fuzzy or cross-file ("how did we fix X", "what did we decide about Y", "which skill handles Z"). Complements grep (exact) and Serena (code symbols). If the user asks a recall question that doesn't map to a specific known file, reach here first.

entries.name: Adt Release Flow
entries.description: Ship validated changes with repeatable release evidence — version bump, changelog, tag, and optional GitHub release

entries.name: Adt Repo Intake
entries.description: Onboard into an unfamiliar repository fast before making any changes

entries.name: Adt Research
entries.description: Deep research on a topic using web search, docs, and codebase analysis

entries.name: Adt Resume
entries.description: Recover session state from git, plans, and open PRs, then continue from the last completed phase without repeating work

entries.name: Adt Route
entries.description: Route a task to the most fitting skill or agent. Parses user intent, checks installed-skill inventory, and picks the best match (single skill or chain).

entries.name: Adt Schedule
entries.description: Define and manage recurring automated agent runs — CI monitoring, dep updates, security scans

entries.name: Adt Secure
entries.description: Shortcut for security review on current change set. Runs layered checks (secret-scan, dep-audit, semgrep, OWASP patterns, prompt-injection review).

entries.name: Adt Self Heal
entries.description: Autonomous error recovery — detect failures, diagnose root cause, apply fixes, and resume without stopping

entries.name: Adt Ship
entries.description: Commit, push, and open a pull request with a conventional commit message

entries.name: Adt Ship Check
entries.description: Verify work before committing, pushing, or requesting review

entries.name: Adt Specs Aggregate Roadmap
entries.description: Regenerate the cross-repo roadmap aggregate in your Claude memory directory by walking every curated repo's `docs/specs/**` frontmatter. Run at session start for cross-repo orientation or after any `spec-new`/`spec-ship` in any repo.

entries.name: Adt Specs Roadmap Refresh
entries.description: Regenerate `docs/roadmap.md` for a repo from all specs under `docs/specs/` + their frontmatter (status, tags, pr). Produces Now/Next/Recently-shipped sections. Run after creating / shipping a spec, or at the start of a new session for orientation.

entries.name: Adt Specs Spec New
entries.description: Create a committed per-feature spec under `docs/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>/{spec,tasks}.md` with frontmatter (status, created, owner, pr, tags). Promotes an ephemeral `.claude/plans/<name>.md` into a persistent, searchable artifact. Use when starting a feature that's larger than a one-shot PR.

entries.name: Adt Sync PT Parity
entries.description: Sync files (skills/patterns/agents/specs) from EN canonical repo to PT mirror, inserting "Tradução pendente" blockquote after YAML frontmatter, then open PR.

entries.name: Adt Tdd
entries.description: Test-driven development — write tests first, implement second, refactor third

entries.name: Adt Ticket
entries.description: Assemble a Jira/Linear/GitHub-Issue body from recent commits, diff, and branch context

entries.name: Adt Toolkit Sync
entries.description: Bump the ai-dev-toolkit version pin in a downstream consumer repo and verify the sync path

entries.name: Adt Verify
entries.description: Run full quality gate suite before committing or creating a PR

entries.name: Adt Worktree Flow
entries.description: Decide when and how to use git worktrees for isolated parallel work

entries.name: AI Agent Gotchas
entries.description: AI coding agents fail in consistent, predictable ways. They fabricate npm packages that don't exist. They catch errors and continue silently. They add features you never asked for. They lose context mid-session and forget what they were doing.

entries.name: Algorithmic Art
entries.description: Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. Use this when users request creating art using code, generative art, algorithmic art, flow fields, or particle systems. Create original algorithmic art rather than copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.

entries.name: Brainstorming
entries.description: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.

entries.name: Brand Guidelines
entries.description: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to any sort of artifact that may benefit from having Anthropic's look-and-feel. Use it when brand colors or style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards apply.

entries.name: Canvas Design
entries.description: Create beautiful visual art in .png and .pdf documents using design philosophy. You should use this skill when the user asks to create a poster, piece of art, design, or other static piece. Create original visual designs, never copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.

entries.name: Claude Api
entries.description: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements). TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (O

entries.name: AI-Assisted Code Review
entries.description: Code review is time-consuming and inconsistent. Reviewers miss security issues, overlook edge cases, or focus on style over substance. Meanwhile, AI agents catch these mechanical issues instantly but can't judge business logic correctness, UX decisions, or architectural fit.

entries.name: Context Building
entries.description: AI agents are only as good as the context they receive. A powerful model with zero project context produces generic code. A mediocre model with excellent context produces code that fits your project perfectly.

entries.name: Dispatching Parallel Agents
entries.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

entries.name: Doc Coauthoring
entries.description: Guide users through a structured workflow for co-authoring documentation. Use when user wants to write documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision docs, or similar structured content. This workflow helps users efficiently transfer context, refine content through iteration, and verify the doc works for readers. Trigger when user mentions writing docs, creating proposals, drafting specs, or similar documentation tasks.

entries.name: Docx
entries.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments,

entries.name: Eng Api Design Reviewer
entries.description: API Design Reviewer

entries.name: Eng Api Test Suite Builder
entries.description: Use when the user asks to generate API tests, create integration test suites, test REST endpoints, or build contract tests.

entries.name: Eng CI CD Pipeline Builder
entries.description: CI/CD Pipeline Builder

entries.name: Eng Code Tour
entries.description: Use when the user asks to create a CodeTour .tour file — persona-targeted, step-by-step walkthroughs that link to real files and line numbers. Trigger for: create a tour, onboarding tour, architecture tour, PR review tour, explain how X works, vibe check, RCA tour, contributor guide, or any structured code walkthrough request.

entries.name: Eng Codebase Onboarding
entries.description: Codebase Onboarding

entries.name: Eng Database Designer
entries.description: Use when the user asks to design database schemas, plan data migrations, optimize queries, choose between SQL and NoSQL, or model data relationships.

entries.name: Eng Dependency Auditor
entries.description: Dependency Auditor

entries.name: Eng Docker Development
entries.description: Docker and container development agent skill and plugin for Dockerfile optimization, docker-compose orchestration, multi-stage builds, and container security hardening. Use when: user wants to optimize a Dockerfile, create or improve docker-compose configurations, implement multi-stage builds, audit container security, reduce image size, or follow container best practices. Covers build performance, layer caching, secret management, and production-ready container patterns.

entries.name: Eng Env Secrets Manager
entries.description: Env & Secrets Manager

entries.name: Eng Focused Fix
entries.description: Use when the user asks to fix, debug, or make a specific feature/module/area work end-to-end. Triggers: 'make X work', 'fix the Y feature', 'the Z module is broken', 'focus on [area]'. Not for quick single-bug fixes — this is for systematic deep-dive repair across all files and dependencies.

entries.name: Eng Git Worktree Manager
entries.description: Git Worktree Manager

entries.name: Eng Mcp Server Builder
entries.description: MCP Server Builder

entries.name: Executing Plans
entries.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

entries.name: Finishing A Development Branch
entries.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup

entries.name: Frontend Design
entries.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

entries.name: Git Worktrees for Concurrent AI Agent Work
entries.description: Multiple AI agent sessions working on the same repository cause chaos. Session A is on branch feature/auth. Session B switches to main to check something. Session A's next tool call fails because the branch disappeared. Or worse: Session B force-pushes over Session A's work.

entries.name: Internal Comms
entries.description: A set of resources to help me write all kinds of internal communications, using the formats that my company likes to use. Claude should use this skill whenever asked to write some sort of internal communications (status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, project updates, etc.).

entries.name: Mcp Builder
entries.description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).

entries.name: Pdf
entries.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.

entries.name: Pptx
entries.description: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slid

entries.name: Prompt Injection Defense: Layered Strategies
entries.description: Prompt injection is not a theoretical attack. By Q1 2026, the benchmark is real: CaMeL achieves 67% injection block rate on AgentDojo. NIST AI RMF frames it as agent hijacking. Defense is not a single gate; it's depth.

entries.name: Prompting Discipline
entries.description: How you ask the model matters more than which model you ask. Two disciplines stop most AI coding failures before they start.

entries.name: Receiving Code Review
entries.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation

entries.name: Requesting Code Review
entries.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements

entries.name: Skill Creator
entries.description: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.

entries.name: SKILL.md Adoption: Vendor-Neutral Skill Discovery
entries.description: SKILL.md is an open specification released by Anthropic in 2025 that standardizes how tools discover and auto-invoke procedural knowledge (skills, patterns, guides, rules). Instead of each tool (Claude, Cursor, VSCode, CLI) maintaining isolated skill registries, SKILL.md enables a single skill to be published once and auto-invoked across any tool that speaks the spec.

entries.name: Slack Gif Creator
entries.description: Knowledge and utilities for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack. Provides constraints, validation tools, and animation concepts. Use when users request animated GIFs for Slack like "make me a GIF of X doing Y for Slack."

entries.name: Spec Driven Development
entries.description: AI agents are fast at writing code but have no memory of your intent. You describe a feature in a prompt, the agent builds it, and three sessions later a different agent (or you) changes it in a way that drifts from the original requirement. The spec lives only in your head.

entries.name: Subagent Driven Development
entries.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

entries.name: Systematic Debugging
entries.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

entries.name: Task Orchestration
entries.description: You have 5 projects, each with a session. You open each one, paste "continue with next priorities", wait, switch, repeat. You're acting as a human cron job.

entries.name: Test Driven Development
entries.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

entries.name: AI-Assisted Test Generation
entries.description: Test generation is repetitive but critical. Developers skip tests because writing edge cases is tedious. AI agents can generate tests quickly but often produce brittle, unrealistic tests that provide false confidence. Tests that mock internals, use fake data like "foo@example.com", or test implementation details break on every refactor.

entries.name: Theme Factory
entries.description: Toolkit for styling artifacts with a theme. These artifacts can be slides, docs, reportings, HTML landing pages, etc. There are 10 pre-set themes with colors/fonts that you can apply to any artifact that has been creating, or can generate a new theme on-the-fly.

entries.name: Using Git Worktrees
entries.description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification

entries.name: Using Superpowers
entries.description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions

entries.name: Verification Before Completion
entries.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always

entries.name: Web Artifacts Builder
entries.description: Suite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui). Use for complex artifacts requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components - not for simple single-file HTML/JSX artifacts.

entries.name: Webapp Testing
entries.description: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.

entries.name: Writing Plans
entries.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

entries.name: Writing Skills
entries.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

entries.name: Xlsx
entries.description: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my
